must_detect.matched_text:
Project Fjord — Indicative Term Sheet for the proposed acquisition of Novaplace Digital Custody SA by AuroraGrid NV and concurrent issue of green-linked convertible notes.
Parties: AuroraGrid NV (incorporated in Meridia, EU) as Issuer/Buyer; Novaplace Digital Custody SA (incorporated in Luxeron, EU) as Target; select subscribers including Silver Reef Capital SCSp and Northbridge Renewables Fund I (each a professional investor in the EU).
Consideration: Fixed cash price of €184,000,000 on a locked-box basis as of 31 March 2026 plus earn-out up to €26,000,000 tied to (i) Target’s EU MiCA-compliant custodial assets under custody and (ii) AuroraGrid’s Scope 2 emissions intensity reduction aligned with CSRD taxonomy KPIs.
Financing: €150,000,000 senior unsecured green-linked convertible notes due 2032, 2.25% cash coupon with +/- 50 bps ratchet based on audited CSRD climate disclosures (SBTi alignment and EU Taxonomy eligible capex), initial conversion price at 27% premium to VWAP subject to standard anti-dilution.
Listing/Prospectus: If notes are admitted to the Borealis Regulated Market (BRM) in Meridia, a prospectus compliant with EU rules shall be approved by the Competent Authority of Meridia (Autoridad de Mercados de Meridia, AMM); otherwise, a private placement to qualified investors only with selling restrictions in each Fictional Member State.
Non-public cyber incident: Target confirms a confidential security incident identified on 28 May 2026 affecting a cold-storage key management module; remediation and third-party forensics are ongoing and are treated as inside information under EU MAR pending public disclosure.
Digital asset operations: Target plans a non-public migration of certain high-value stablecoin custody wallets from shard scheme “Nova-3” to “Nova-5” with revised seed-phrase quorum; potential temporary suspension of retail inflows is under consideration and could be price-sensitive if disclosed.
ESG/climate diligence: AuroraGrid’s draft CSRD datapack (not yet public) indicates a methodology change for location-based electricity factors that may alter previously communicated carbon intensity figures and trigger the coupon ratchet; assurance by an independent auditor is in progress.
Market Abuse controls: “Project Fjord” insider list will be established and maintained in real time, including Mr. Julian Voss (CFO) and Ms. Elina Corbet (General Counsel), with segregation of functions, watchlist and restricted list protocols, and PDMR blackout windows consistent with EU MAR; no selective disclosure or tipping is permitted.
GDPR and data export: Data room access limited to necessity; special category data under GDPR Art 9 shall be avoided and, if unavoidable for HR or conduct checks, processed under appropriate derogations with minimisation, SCCs, and transfer impact assessments for hosting by CloudHenge Services Ltd. in the Ardora Islands (non-EEA).
Information barriers: AuroraGrid’s proprietary crypto trading desk and cyber response unit will be ring-fenced from the M&A team with recorded wall-crossing procedures and pre-clearance of personal transactions; all communications to counterparties will be channelled through designated counsel only.
Conditions precedent: Completion is subject to (i) AMM non-objection for delayed disclosure under EU MAR Art 17(4), (ii) Target’s CASP authorisation update under MiCA in Meridia and Luxeron, (iii) merger clearance from the Meridian Competition Authority, and (iv) no material adverse change, including recurrence of high-severity cyber incidents.
Commercial terms and warranties: Standard warranties on title, regulatory authorisations, crypto key management controls, and ESG disclosures; indemnity cap at 15% of enterprise value, de minimis €50,000, basket €1,500,000, warranty and tax claim survival 24/60 months respectively.
Announcements and communications: No announcement shall be made prior to registration with the Meridia-OAM; all external statements must be approved by Ms. Elina Corbet at elina.corbet@auroragrid.example.eu, and access to the virtual data room is via fjord.dealroom@auroragrid.example.eu.
Exclusivity, standstill, and governing law: 60-day exclusivity and standstill apply; this term sheet is non-binding except for confidentiality, costs, governing law (Meridia), jurisdiction (courts of North Meris), and the provisions on anno
